project.name: Microsphere Java
project.description: A foundational Java library providing reusable components, utilities, and annotation processing capabilities for the MicroSphere ecosystem.

nodes.name: maven-build.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds and tests the Java project with Maven across multiple JDK versions (8, 11, 17, 21, 25) on push and pull_request events, with Codecov coverage upload.

nodes.name: maven-publish.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that publishes the project to Maven Central, generates release notes via GitHub Copilot, creates a GitHub release, increments the patch version, and merges release back into main.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds an inline Python script invoking the GitHub Models API (GPT-4o) to generate AI-powered release notes from git history.

nodes.name: merge-main-to-branches.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that automatically merges the main branch into dev and release branches whenever changes are pushed to main, using a matrix strategy for parallel merges.

nodes.name: sync-branches-from-upstream.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that detects if the repository is a fork, then syncs all branches from the upstream parent repository, running on push events or every 5 minutes via cron schedule.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses concurrency grouping to cancel in-progress runs and includes fork-detection logic via the GitHub API before proceeding with branch synchronization.

nodes.name: wiki-publish.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that generates Markdown wiki documentation from Java source files using a Python script and publishes the results to the project's GitHub Wiki repository.

nodes.name: create-readme.prompt.md
nodes.summary: GitHub Copilot prompt template instructing AI agents to generate comprehensive README.md files for the project, covering essential sections, content structure guidelines, and technical requirements for GitHub Flavored Markdown.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses YAML frontmatter for agent configuration and GitHub Flavored Markdown for structured prompt instructions.

nodes.name: document-api.prompt.md
nodes.summary: GitHub Copilot prompt template guiding AI agents to produce OpenAPI 3.0.3 specifications from API endpoint code, including path definitions, parameter schemas, response models, and reusable components.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses YAML frontmatter with agent and description fields, plus ${input:...} template variables for dynamic user input.

nodes.name: explain-code.prompt.md
nodes.summary: Concise GitHub Copilot prompt template directing AI agents to explain code snippets in a beginner-friendly manner with step-by-step breakdowns, key concept clarifications, and practical usage examples.

nodes.name: generate-unit-tests.prompt.md
nodes.summary: GitHub Copilot prompt template for generating unit tests with a structured strategy covering core functionality, input validation, error handling, and side effects, following the AAA (Arrange-Act-Assert) pattern.
nodes.languageNotes: Employs template variables for dynamic function name and testing framework selection.

nodes.name: onboarding-plan.prompt.md
nodes.summary: GitHub Copilot prompt template that creates a structured phased onboarding plan for new team members joining the Microsphere Projects organization, covering foundation setup, codebase exploration, and team integration phases.

nodes.name: review-code.prompt.md
nodes.summary: GitHub Copilot prompt template instructing AI agents to perform comprehensive code reviews covering security vulnerabilities, performance optimization, code quality, architecture design, and testing with detailed actionable feedback.

nodes.name: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
nodes.summary: Standard code of conduct document outlining community behavior expectations, reporting guidelines, and enforcement policies for contributors to the Microsphere Java project.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Main project overview with installation instructions, module listing, usage examples for string, collection, reflection, type conversion, and event dispatching utilities, along with build guide and contribution information.

nodes.name: mvnw.cmd
nodes.summary: Apache Maven Wrapper batch script (version 3.3.4) that automatically downloads and invokes the correct Maven version for building the project, with dual Windows batch and PowerShell implementation.

nodes.name: onboarding-plan.md
nodes.summary: Structured onboarding guide for new contributors with three phases covering environment setup, module exploration, and team integration, spanning the first days through week 3 of joining the project.

nodes.name: pom.xml
nodes.summary: Root Maven POM defining the multi-module Microsphere Java project with 8 sub-modules (dependencies, annotations, core, JDK tools, test, lang-model, annotation-processor), using revision-based versioning and inheriting from the microsphere-build parent POM.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Maven CI-friendly revision property placeholder for versioning with a single ${revision} property.

nodes.name: release-notes.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive release history from version 0.2.7 through 0.3.6, documenting new features, bug fixes, dependency updates, test improvements, and build workflow enhancements across the project lifecycle.

nodes.name: user-guide.md
nodes.summary: In-depth user guide covering all 14 core packages of the Microsphere Java framework with code examples, architecture explanations, design patterns, and advanced topics for intermediate and seasoned developers.

layers.name: Core Foundation
layers.description: Primary library module providing reusable Java utilities including collections, reflection, I/O, events, type conversion, filtering, logging, JSON processing, concurrent primitives, and string/classloader utilities.

layers.name: Annotation Definitions
layers.description: Custom Java annotation definitions including @Since, @Experimental, @Immutable, @Nonnull, @Nullable, @Order, @Priority, @Version, @ConfigurationProperty, @Suppress, ThreadSafe, and NotThreadSafe annotations for the Microsphere ecosystem.

layers.name: Annotation Processing
layers.description: Compile-time annotation processors that generate JSON metadata and configuration property files from @ConfigurationProperty annotations during compilation.

layers.name: Language Model
layers.description: Java language model utilities for annotation processing including type/element/method/field resolution utilities, JSON annotation value visitors, and element comparators used by the annotation processors.

layers.name: Test Support
layers.description: Testing infrastructure providing abstract annotation processing tests, compiler invocation interception, and reusable model classes for validating reflection, generics, and configuration property behavior across all modules.

layers.name: JDK Tools
layers.description: JDK compiler and tool provider abstractions for compiling Java source programmatically and discovering available JDK tools.

layers.name: Build Configuration
layers.description: Maven build configuration including root and module POMs, Maven wrapper scripts, dependency BOM, parent POM, Dependabot config, and tool metadata for the Microsphere Java multi-module project.

layers.name: CI/CD Pipeline
layers.description: GitHub Actions automation workflows for building across multiple JDK versions, publishing to Maven Central, synchronizing branches, generating wiki documentation, and the Python wiki documentation generation script.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation including README, user guide, release notes, code of conduct, onboarding plan, and GitHub Copilot prompt templates for code review, test generation, onboarding, API documentation, and code explanation.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README introduces Microsphere Java Framework as a foundational library for the MicroSphere ecosystem. It describes the project's 8 modules, key features like string utilities, reflection, type conversion, event dispatching, and annotation processing, and explains how to integrate the library via Maven or Gradle. This document provides the roadmap for understanding the entire codebase.

tour.title: Build Architecture
tour.description: The root pom.xml is the entry point for the multi-module Maven build. It aggregates 8 modules including microsphere-java-core, microsphere-java-annotations, microsphere-annotation-processor, microsphere-lang-model, microsphere-jdk-tools, microsphere-java-test, microsphere-java-dependencies, and microsphere-java-parent. Understanding this structure reveals how the framework decomposes into focused, reusable libraries that can be consumed independently.
tour.languageLesson: Maven multi-module POMs use a parent/child hierarchy. The root POM declares <modules> for aggregation and <dependencyManagement> for version consistency across submodules, while each child inherits shared build configuration.

tour.title: Core Utility Primitives
tour.description: The core module provides essential utility classes that form the foundation of the entire framework. StringUtils offers null-safe string operations, CollectionUtils provides collection manipulation helpers, FileUtils handles file I/O, and URLUtils works with URL parsing and construction. These utilities are designed to be dependency-free and usable in any Java project, embodying the framework's philosophy of providing building blocks that accelerate development.
tour.languageLesson: Java utility classes typically expose only static methods and have a private constructor to prevent instantiation. Null-safe design patterns (defensive checks at method boundaries) are a core idiom used throughout this codebase.

tour.title: Reflection Utilities and Prioritization
tour.description: FieldUtils provides simplified reflective access to object fields, supporting both public and private members. The Prioritized interface establishes a consistent ordering contract for components, enabling sorted iteration and precedence-based selection. Together, these classes represent the framework's approach to Java reflection: wrapping the verbose java.lang.reflect API into concise, reusable operations.
tour.languageLesson: The Prioritized interface follows the Comparable pattern commonly seen in Spring Framework. By returning an integer priority value, components can be ordered in lists, enabling a plugin architecture where higher-priority implementations override lower-priority ones.

tour.title: SPI-Based Extensibility: Converters and Events
tour.description: The Converters class defines a ServiceLoader-based SPI for type conversion, allowing the framework to convert between arbitrary Java types at runtime. EventDispatcher provides a lightweight event system with both direct (synchronous) and parallel execution strategies. Both classes demonstrate the framework's commitment to extensibility: new converters or event listeners can be registered via META-INF/services files without modifying existing code.
tour.languageLesson: Java ServiceLoader enables plugin discovery at runtime. By placing a file named after the interface in META-INF/services, implementations are automatically discovered and loaded, providing a standard-JDK alternative to dependency injection containers.

tour.title: Annotation Contracts
tour.description: The @ConfigurationProperty annotation defines the contract for mapping Java class fields to external configuration properties. It belongs to the annotations module, which houses all custom annotation definitions used by the framework. This annotation acts as the bridge between the framework's annotation processing pipeline and the runtime configuration system, marking fields that should participate in property binding.

tour.title: Language Model Type Resolution
tour.description: The TypeUtils class from the language model module provides utilities for resolving Java types using the Java Language Model API (javax.lang.model). It handles parameterized types, type variables, and annotation mirror resolution -- operations essential for annotation processing. This module acts as the bridge between the raw annotation definitions from Step 6 and the compile-time processing logic in Step 8.
tour.languageLesson: The javax.lang.model API represents Java source code as elements (packages, classes, methods) and types (declared types, type variables, wildcards). TypeUtils abstracts this API into convenient methods, handling edge cases like nested generics and array types.

tour.title: Compile-Time Annotation Processing
tour.description: The ConfigurationPropertyGenerator processes @ConfigurationProperty annotations at compile time, generating JSON metadata files that describe the configuration properties in a machine-readable format. This file is consumed by the runtime configuration system to validate and document available properties. The annotation processor demonstrates the framework's use of the javax.annotation.processing API for code generation without runtime overhead.
tour.languageLesson: Java annotation processors implement javax.annotation.processing.Processor and are registered via META-INF/services/javax.annotation.processing.Processor. They run during compilation, before the final .class files are written, enabling code generation and validation at build time.

tour.title: Programmatic Compilation
tour.description: The Compiler class from the JDK tools module abstracts the javax.tools.JavaCompiler API, providing a programmatic interface for compiling Java source code at runtime. This capability supports the annotation processing pipeline by enabling compiled outputs from generated sources to be integrated into the build without manual compilation steps.
tour.languageLesson: javax.tools.JavaCompiler provides programmatic access to the same compiler used by javac. It accepts a JavaFileManager and DiagnosticListener for fine-grained control over compilation, enabling in-memory compilation of generated source code.

tour.title: Test Infrastructure for Annotation Processing
tour.description: The AbstractAnnotationProcessingTest provides a reusable base class for testing annotation processors in a JUnit 5 environment. It sets up the javax.annotation.processing infrastructure needed to invoke processors programmatically and verify their output. This class completes the framework's annotation processing story by showing how the full pipeline -- from annotations through language model utilities through processor through compilation -- is verified end-to-end.
